components.title: Identity Provider (Microsoft Entra ID)
components.description: Cloud identity provider (Microsoft Entra ID, hybrid-joined with on-premises Active Directory) providing single sign-on and conditional access enforcement across the organisation's cloud services, SaaS applications, and any on-premises applications federated via SAML/OIDC. Enforces phishing-resistant multi-factor authentication for all users and blocks legacy authentication protocols that cannot support MFA. This is distinct from on-premises Active Directory, which provides domain-joined device authentication and Group Policy — Entra ID is the identity plane for cloud and SSO-federated access.
components.purpose: Provide centralised single sign-on and conditional-access-enforced authentication across cloud services and federated applications, with multi-factor authentication enforced consistently regardless of which application a user is signing into.

props.name: deployment-model
props.value: Cloud identity provider, hybrid-joined with on-premises Active Directory via Entra Connect (or Entra ID-only for cloud-native tenants)

props.name: sso-protocols
props.value: SAML 2.0, OpenID Connect / OAuth 2.0 for federated application single sign-on

props.name: mfa-method
props.value: Phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2 security keys, Windows Hello for Business, Certificate-based Authentication) as the primary method

props.name: conditional-access
props.value: Conditional Access policies enforcing MFA, device compliance, and location/risk-based access controls for all cloud application sign-in

protocols.name: https
protocols.title: HTTPS — Authentication endpoints, admin portal, Conditional Access policy evaluation

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for the Identity Provider (Microsoft Entra ID).

implemented-requirements.description: Multi-factor authentication is enforced via Conditional Access policy for all users authenticating to the organisation's online services and SSO-federated applications that process, store, or communicate sensitive organisational data.

Multi-factor authentication for user access to online services is phishing-resistant, enforced via Conditional Access Authentication Strength policies requiring FIDO2 security keys, Windows Hello for Business, or Certificate-based Authentication rather than SMS or voice OTP.

Legacy authentication protocols that do not support multi-factor authentication (Basic Auth, legacy MAPI/RPC, POP3/IMAP4 without modern auth) are blocked at the identity provider for all cloud service access via a dedicated Conditional Access policy targeting the 'Other clients' condition.

Multi-factor authentication is required for privileged users (Global Administrators, Conditional Access administrators, and other directory role holders) authenticating to the identity provider's administrative portal, with a stricter Authentication Strength requirement than standard users.

Directory role assignments (privileged access within the identity provider itself) are limited to the minimum required for each administrator's function, using Privileged Identity Management (PIM) for just-in-time, time-bound elevation rather than standing role assignments.

Successful and unsuccessful multi-factor authentication events are centrally logged via the identity provider's sign-in logs and forwarded to the SIEM, supporting detection of credential-stuffing and MFA-fatigue attack patterns.
Privileged access events within the identity provider (directory role assignment changes, Conditional Access policy changes, PIM activations) are centrally logged and forwarded to the SIEM.
